title: broadcast payment msgs → non-involved peers emit spurious Unknown replies
description:
Payment messages are sent via Broadcast (all room members), by design for relay blindness. In a room with N>2 users, every non-involved peer receives each payment step, dispatches it as unknown-session+non-Request, and broadcasts a PaymentAction::Unknown reply.

The relay uses broadcast(Some(sender), msg) which EXCLUDES the sender — confirmed in relay/src/ws.rs:229 and state.rs:102. So C's Unknown goes to A and B, both hit the Occupied path (logs warning, returns true, sends nothing). No feedback loop: the chain terminates after exactly 1 Unknown per non-involved party per payment step.

Current risk: bounded noise — N-2 spurious Unknown messages per payment step.
Future risk (when Occupied handlers are added): if those handlers don't validate from==peer_pub_id, an Unknown from a non-party C could drive the payer's state machine with an event it was not meant to receive. Each Occupied handler added in future issues MUST check from before acting on the action.
